title: Xeraea riedelii
intro: Xeraea riedelii, also known as Riedel's tree or Xeraea, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. Xeraea riedelii contains bioactive constituents such as limonoids, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being formulated into capsules or tinctures for convenient consumption.
